message:
[medium] hubspot: reject base_url overrides with query, fragment, or userinfo

validate_base_url_for_auth pinned the host to api.hubapi.com /
api.hubspot.com in direct-token mode and accepted any https URL in
credential_id mode, but returned parsed.to_string().trim_end_matches('/')
— query, fragment, and userinfo components survived into HubSpotClient
which then concatenates via format!("{}{path}", self.base_url) in every
request method (client.rs:184/195/210/221/232). A configure payload like

    {"access_token": "pat-na1-x", "base_url": "https://api.hubapi.com/?leak=x"}

would pass validation and leak `?leak=x` on every HubSpot API request,
with the endpoint path concatenated after the `?` boundary where it
parses as part of the query. Userinfo
(`https://attacker:pw@api.hubapi.com/`) would bake into every request
URL and silently override the bearer token the connector sets via the
Authorization header.

- validate_base_url_for_auth now rejects userinfo (username or password)
  and query/fragment components before the host-allowlist / scheme
  checks, in both BearerToken and CredentialId modes.
- Six new unit tests: accept clean api.hubapi.com (happy path), Token
  reject (query / fragment / userinfo), CredentialId reject (query),
  and a substring-smuggle regression verifying
  `https://evil.com/api.hubapi.com/` is rejected because the parsed
  host is evil.com, not api.hubapi.com.

Verification: CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/fcp-review-p7 cargo test -p
fcp-hubspot --lib → 213 passed; 0 failed (207 prior + 6 new). Matches
the hygiene pattern already shipped in airtable / notion / asana /
gmail / whatsapp / linear.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
